messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: @CSP-Tom and I have been working on a wardriving app with multi-region support: https://meshmapper.net/ Requirement: you’ll need an existing MeshCore Packet Analyzer Observer running. Huge thanks to @🌲 Tree for making this possible. How it works Frontend: a BLE WebApp that performs the wardriving (originally a fork) Backend: stores/organizes the data + collects packets from MeshCore Packet Analyzer Demo (Ottawa / YOW — currently the most populated) Map: https://yow.meshmapper.net/ Leaderboard: https://yow.meshmapper.net/leaderboard.php Try this: Click on a repeater Explore the layers Noise Heatmap is new Check the leaderboards Click on various coverage blocks Features Administration controls: limit wardrive sessions, disable wardriving entirely, or allow RX-only (passive) wardriving per region Collect noise floor data and map city-wide noise Graph multiple types of coverage zones Graph repeater coverage Track how far repeaters can reach Plus a bunch of other things I’m probably forgetting Repeaters + duplicates (important) If your region has unique repeater IDs, you’ll get some very cool repeater visualizations/stats. If your region has duplicate repeater IDs, the app still works, but it will exclude pings from repeater stats for IDs that are known duplicates (to keep the data sane). Want your region onboarded? Head here https://meshmapper.net/?onboarding and fill out the web form! Want to request a feature/report and issue. https://github.com/MeshMapper/MeshMapper_Project (edited)
messages.content_html:
@CSP-Tom and I have been working on a wardriving app with multi-region support: https://meshmapper.net/ Requirement: you’ll need an existing MeshCore Packet Analyzer Observer running. Huge thanks to @🌲 Tree for making this possible. How it works
  • Frontend: a BLE WebApp that performs the wardriving (originally a fork)
  • Backend: stores/organizes the data + collects packets from MeshCore Packet Analyzer Demo (Ottawa / YOW — currently the most populated)
Map: https://yow.meshmapper.net/ Leaderboard: https://yow.meshmapper.net/leaderboard.php Try this:
  • Click on a repeater
  • Explore the layers
    • Noise Heatmap is new
  • Check the leaderboards
  • Click on various coverage blocks Features
  • Administration controls: limit wardrive sessions, disable wardriving entirely, or allow RX-only (passive) wardriving per region
  • Collect noise floor data and map city-wide noise
  • Graph multiple types of coverage zones
  • Graph repeater coverage
  • Track how far repeaters can reach
  • Plus a bunch of other things I’m probably forgetting Repeaters + duplicates (important)
  • If your region has unique repeater IDs, you’ll get some very cool repeater visualizations/stats.
  • If your region has duplicate repeater IDs, the app still works, but it will exclude pings from repeater stats for IDs that are known duplicates (to keep the data sane). Want your region onboarded?
Head here https://meshmapper.net/?onboarding and fill out the web form! Want to request a feature/report and issue. https://github.com/MeshMapper/MeshMapper_Project (edited)

messages.text: MeshMapper – Wardrive Client WebApp (v1.8.4) New: Carpeater Filter Ignore by ID: Enter a 2‑char hex repeater ID to silently drop all packets from that repeater (TX + RX). RSSI failsafe: Drops unusually strong signals from other repeaters and shows a single running drop counter in the Error Log. Persistence: Added persistence for the last-used value and setting using browser storage (retains values across sessions unless site/browser data is cleared). Minor Added new device models for Seeed Wio Tracker L1 and Seeed Tracker T1000 . (edited)
messages.content_html:

  • Ignore by ID: Enter a 2‑char hex repeater ID to silently drop all packets from that repeater (TX + RX).
  • RSSI failsafe: Drops unusually strong signals from other repeaters and shows a single running drop counter in the Error Log.
  • Persistence: Added persistence for the last-used value and setting using browser storage (retains values across sessions unless site/browser data is cleared).
Minor
  • Added new device models for Seeed Wio Tracker L1 and Seeed Tracker T1000.
 (edited)

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: Before you start Back up your companion device. Advert your companion on the Mesh, its how we authenicate you. This only needs to happen once every 60 days Android 1. Flood advert your companion using the MeshCore App 2. Close/disconnect the MeshCore app. 3. Open Google Chrome and go to: https://wardrive.meshmapper.net/ 4. Connect your device to the page. 5. Choose your scan interval and if you’re using and external antenna or not. 6. Send a ping or enable Auto TX/RX(Active Wardrive) or Auto RX(Passive Wardrive). 7. Walk/drive around and watch the tracking live. iOS 1. Flood advert your companion using the MeshCore App 2. Close/disconnect the MeshCore app. 3. Install Bluefy (Web BLE browser): https://apps.apple.com/us/app/bluefy-web-ble-browser/id1492822055 4. Open https://wardrive.meshmapper.net/ inside Bluefy. 5. Connect your device to the page. 6. Choose your scan interval and if you’re using and external antenna or not. 7. Send a ping or enable Auto TX/RX(Active Wardrive) or Auto RX(Passive Wardrive). 8. Move around and watch the tracking live. Native App Closed Beta If you'd like access to the iOS or Android closed beta, DM me your app store email address. (edited)

messages.reply_html:
MrAlders0n
Before you start Back up your companion device. Advert your companion on the Mesh, its how we authenicate you. This only needs to happen once every 60 days Android 1. Flood advert your companion using the MeshCore App 2. Close/disconnect the MeshCore app. 3. Open Google Chrome and go to: https://wardrive.meshmapper.net/ 4. Connect your device to the page. 5. Choose your scan interval and if you’re using and external antenna or not. 6. Send a ping or enable Auto TX/RX(Active Wardrive) or Auto RX(Passive Wardrive). 7. Walk/drive around and watch the tracking live. iOS 1. Flood advert your companion using the MeshCore App 2. Close/disconnect the MeshCore app. 3. Install Bluefy (Web BLE browser): https://apps.apple.com/us/app/bluefy-web-ble-browser/id1492822055 4. Open https://wardrive.meshmapper.net/ inside Bluefy. 5. Connect your device to the page. 6. Choose your scan interval and if you’re using and external antenna or not. 7. Send a ping or enable Auto TX/RX(Active Wardrive) or Auto RX(Passive Wardrive). 8. Move around and watch the tracking live. Native App Closed Beta If you'd like access to the iOS or Android closed beta, DM me your app store email address. (edited)

messages.author: CSP-Tom
messages.text: Hey @n30nex The issue stems from your region only having a single observer (which in your case is also a repeater). When it sends an advert, it doesn't hear itself (and there are no other observers to hear it). Letsmesh isn't getting its adverts, so MeshMapper isn't either So what we've done is added a "single observer mode" option, which will bypass all of the repeater aging (and ultimate removal from the map if too old) logic. If one day your region expands with multiple observers, we'll flip this back off.

messages.author: CSP-Tom
messages.text: A little about how duplicate repeaters work with MeshMapper... When a repeater adverts and it's sharing the same 2-digit ID as another, both that repeater and the existing one will enter an "excluded" state. This prevents any calculation of max distance because there is no way to tell which repeater was involved in a ping. New data coming in with that repeater ID involved (it heard a repeat from that ID, that ID was involved as a hop, etc.) will forever be flagged as not knowing exactly where the repeater was, and will never be involved in any max distance or "line drawing" calculations. Duplicate repeaters are checked automatically every day and if one of those repeaters hasn't sent an advert in 3 days, it's removed from the database and the other conflicting repeater (as long as it HAS sent an advert recently) is fully reactivated. You can speed this process up if you're a region administrator, as the admin portal will flag for you any duplicates and allow you to edit accordingly (for instance if you're aware that the duplicate ID was temporary and has since taken on a new ID). (edited)

messages.author: CSP-Tom
messages.text: Its a numerical representation of the coloured squares on the map. Green (bidirectional), Orange (TX), Purple (RX), blue (Discovery)...are considered as "coverage", where Grey (Dead), and Red (Dropped) is not. So if half your map is green, orange, purple, and blue, and the other half grey and red...your coverage will show as 50% (edited)

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: Hey @arti I can definitely make them more visible! One thing to note: the discs display different data than the map tiles. Take a peek at the map legend for details. The app can't tell if a ping was a full BIDIR, only whether it received a response from a repeater. The idea is that the map discs are really just meant to represent your drive session visually, while the web version is better suited for detailed analysis. That said, I'm open to suggestions on how to make the discs easier to see at a glance. Just keep in mind they should look distinct from the map tiles since they're showing different information.

messages.author: MrAlders0n
messages.text: MeshMapper Session Management When you connect to your MeshCore device, MeshMapper acquires a session slot from the regional API. Sessions are renewed automatically whenever you submit data to the API (TX ping results, RX packets, discovery responses). This means active wardriving keeps your session alive naturally. For Active Mode and Passive Mode, there's an additional safeguard: a heartbeat mechanism that renews your session one minute before expiration if no API submissions have occurred. This ensures continuous wardriving sessions don't unexpectedly drop. However, if you're just connected and idle (or only send a single manual ping), the session will expire after the timeout period (~5 minutes from your last API submission). The next action you attempt - starting Active/Passive Mode or sending another ping - will trigger a "session invalid" error, requiring reconnection. This is intentional. Session slots are a limited resource used to enforce slot limits that protect the mesh network from overload. If you're not actively wardriving, MeshMapper releases your slot so other wardrivers in the region can use it. Idle connections don't get to hold slots indefinitely.

messages.author: CSP-Tom
messages.text: Hey @Regs , this is all fixed up. Pings are tied to repeaters using the fixed coords of the repeater at the time of the ping...so if a repeater is relocated, or retired and another repeater with the same ID is brought online, old data doesn't become associated with the location of the new repeater (ex. if repeater A1 is relocated 200km away from it's original location, we don't want every ping before that relocation still tied to that repeater...we're going to get 200km+ distances being calculated). Anyways, small bug in that logics code. Fixed it up...thanks for finding it.
messages.content_html: Hey @Regs , this is all fixed up. Pings are tied to repeaters using the fixed coords of the repeater at the time of the ping...so if a repeater is relocated, or retired and another repeater with the same ID is brought online, old data doesn't become associated with the location of the new repeater (ex. if repeater A1 is relocated 200km away from it's original location, we don't want every ping before that relocation still tied to that repeater...we're going to get 200km+ distances being calculated).
